channel.title: Exxon on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Exxon on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Patriots Portfolio – Friday, April 3, 2026
item.description: This week's Patriots Portfolio — what the 38% Trump tariff means for Guyana's economy, the Wales Gas Plant arbitration fallout, Exxon's Canje exit, and what patriotic investors need to watch.

item.title: Daily Brief – Friday, April 3, 2026
item.description: Your Good Friday morning roundup — Guyana hit with a 38% Trump tariff, government secretly paid US$82M to gas plant contractor, fake tint waiver medical certificates discovered, BOSAI fires the worker who won a pay raise, and Exxon exits the Canje Block.

item.title: De Boys Seh – Thursday, April 2, 2026
item.description: De boys had a lot to say this Thursday. Tint meters, missing Nicaraguans, Venezuelan jails, and Exxon getting compared to Judas. Again.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh – Thursday, April 2, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh supports the tint crackdown, defends the Exxon contract, and is very proud of UG's new medical programme.

item.title: Daily Brief – Thursday, April 2, 2026
item.description: Your Thursday morning roundup — tint crackdown begins, Nicaraguan engineers allegedly flee with $13M in aircraft parts, Venezuelan jail finally releases Guyanese boat captain, and the U.S. Ambassador tells Guyana to leave the Exxon contract alone.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Take: Six Dialysis Centres, A Special Envoy, And Some People Just Won't Give Credit
item.description: Uncle Ramesh sees six new dialysis centres, a US Special Envoy, and cost recovery on seven oil projects — and wonders why people still complaining.

item.title: Wednesday's Guyana Brief: Noem Lands, Exxon Expands, and the Bridge Is Still Sinking
item.description: A fired US official arrives as a Special Envoy, Exxon wants two more oil projects, and the World Bank says Guyana's reform programme has been quietly gutted. Wednesday's 5-minute news circus.

item.title: Patriots Portfolio — March 24, 2026: Oil, Courts, and the Business of Everything
item.description: This week in Guyana's business and economic landscape: Exxon's production push, rising oil prices, the NRF, and what the Mohameds saga means for business confidence.

item.title: Tuesday's Guyana Brief: Wheelbarrows, Oil Greed, and the Death of a Newspaper
item.description: The Mohameds went to the CCJ, Exxon wants more oil, and somewhere in the background, a newspaper quietly died. Tuesday's 5-minute Guyana news circus.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Take: Cash In Hand, Oil On The Rise, And Some People Just Can't Accept A Good Thing
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Toronto is very pleased with the cash grant, very confident in Exxon, and has some thoughts about people who pay court debts in wheelbarrows.

item.title: YouTube Scripts – March 24, 2026: Wheelbarrows, Oil, and the CCJ
item.description: Two HeyGen-ready YouTube scripts for Tuesday March 24, 2026 — 60-second quick brief and 4-minute full episode.

item.title: ☕ Monday Brief: Former Finance Minister Admits Exxon Exploited Us, Venezuela Border Watch, and Pay-To-Party Culture
item.description: Winston Jordan finally admits what everyone knew, PM Phillips says troops are ready for anything Venezuelan, and birthday parties now cost admission.

item.title: 🇬🇾 Uncle Ramesh Responds: Jordan Finally Talking Truth, Border Security Strong, and Mash Coming!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh sets the record straight on who really signed that oil contract, praises border vigilance, and gets ready for Republic Day.
